title: cordia lowryana
intro: Cordia lowryana, also known as the Mexican Cordia, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Cordia lowryana have been found to provide various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin as a paste or ointment.
